task: I’m putting together a simple house checklist for the next few weeks, and I want it to be easy to use if I’m in a hurry. Could you help me sketch a plain version I can fill in without much fuss?

turns.agent_response:
Yes — here’s a plain fill-in version you can copy by hand.

HOUSE CHECKLIST

Address:

Spare key location:

Main water shutoff:

Water shutoff wrench location:

Main electrical panel location:

Gas shutoff location:

Backup heat source:

Filter size:

Furnace / HVAC contact:

Plumber contact:

Electrician contact:

Insurance contact:

Emergency contact 1:

Emergency contact 2:

Freezing risk spots:
Kitchen sink:
Bathroom sink:
Basement lines:
Garage / crawlspace:

Appliances to watch:
Furnace:
Water heater:
Fridge:
Washer:
Dryer:

Notes:
________________________________
________________________________
________________________________
________________________________

If you want, I can also make this into a tighter one-page version with bigger spacing and only the most time-critical items at the top.

user_simulator.text:
That’ll do. Plain enough to use. I’d keep the key stuff up top myself — water shutoff, breaker panel, backup heat, and the numbers. The rest can sit lower on the page.

The bigger spacing version would be useful too, if it stays simple.
